items.title: aptos-core aptos-node-v1.41.5
items.body:
## Who should upgrade

- Validators: **YES**
- Fullnodes: **YES**

## Release Impact
Please note this release will cleanup some stale data that was caused by a bug fixed last October. If your node was bootstrapped before October, it’s expected to see increase disk usage for several hours up to a day. The actual impact to other part of the system (e.g. your proposal failure rate) should be minimal.

If you’ve observed anything abnormal please raise the issue in the communication channel (e.g. slack channel, telegram channel).

## What's Changed
* add support for SLH-DSA-SHA2-128s TXN authenticators by @alinush in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18300
* [move-prover] Properly desugar receiver style functions in let statements by @mkurnikov in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18438
* [move-prover] Adding behavioral predicates to the type checker by @wrwg in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18429
* [State Sync] Bump network message size, and enable time-and-size aware storage reads. by @JoshLind in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18473
* [move-unit-test] More information for unexpected aborts by @calintat in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18432
* [qs] Populate BatchKind for BatchV2 by @ibalajiarun in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18452
* [qs] support pulling OptQuorumStorePayload::V2 by @ibalajiarun in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18453
* [vm] Stack size checks for native calls by @georgemitenkov in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18475
* [cli] Fix MOVE_VM_STEP/MOVE_VM_TRACE for aptos move replay by @wrwg in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18486
* [prover] Support tuple results in spec functions by @wrwg in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18484
* [prover] Add developer documentation for function values verification by @wrwg in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18493
* [move-prover] Implement beh

items.title: Add Shplonked PCS, PCS tests and aptos-dkg refactoring
items.body: ## Description - Major refactor of sigma protocol code and MSM code (removed `IsMsmInput` trait, `PairingTupleHomomorphism`, introduced `CurveGroupTupleHomomorphism`, etc). - Renaming of `aux` and `ctxt` for Fiat-Shamir sigma protocols to `cntxt` - Moved SoK context of `weighted_transcript` and `weighted_transcript_v2` to a separate file. - Changed SoK context code from tuple into a structure, and moved it from the two chunky's to a separate file called `verify_common` to reduce code duplication - Wrote a simple `domain_separate_dsts()` function to reduce code duplication - New test `tests/pcs.rs` - Added Shplonked PCS, see [here](https://waamm.github.io/posts/shplonked/). - Merged MSMs in verifier of `chunky_v1`, speed effect is quite significant! Before a similar (and as you can see less significant) MSM combination was done for the G_2-part in `chunky_v2`: After some small changes, plus this new change to the MSMs in the verifier of `chunky_v1`: ## How Has This Been Tested? - Existing tests. - New test `tests/pcs.rs` ## Key Areas to Review ## Type of Chan

items.title: Delete node-checker code
items.body:
## Description
This PR entirely deletes the `ecosystem/node-checker` directory and all its contents, including:
*   The `aptos-node-checker` Rust crate
*   The `fn-check-client` sub-crate
*   The TypeScript client (`ts-client/`)
*   Associated configuration examples and OpenAPI spec documentation.

All references to `node-checker` across the workspace have also been removed, including entries in `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, and GitHub Actions workflows.

## How Has This Been Tested?
*   Verified that the `ecosystem/node-checker` directory no longer exists.
*   Confirmed that `cargo check` runs successfully, indicating no broken dependencies or build issues.
*   Used `ripgrep` to ensure all explicit references to `node-checker`, `aptos-node-checker`, and `fn-check-client` have been removed from the codebase.

## Key Areas to Review
*   **`Cargo.toml`**: Ensure all workspace member entries and dependency declarations related to `node-checker` are correctly removed.
*   **`Cargo.lock`**: Verify that the regenerated `Cargo.lock` file contains no entries referencing the deleted crates.
*   **`.github/actions/`**: Confirm that the `--exclude aptos-node-checker` flags have been removed from the `rust-unit-tests` and `rust-doc-tests` actions.
*   **`docker/builder/README.md`**: Check for the removal of the `aptos-fn-check-client` binary mention.

## Type of Change
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Breaking change
- [ ] Performance improvement
- [x] Refactoring
- [ ] Dependency update
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Tests

## Which Components or Systems Does This Change Impact?
- [ ] Validator Node
- [ ] Full Node (API, Indexer, etc.)
- [ ] Move/Aptos Virtual Machine
- [ ] Aptos Framework
- [ ] Aptos CLI/SDK
- [ ] Developer Infrastructure
- [ ] Move Compiler
- [x] Other (Ecosystem tooling - Node Checker)

## Checklist
- [x] I have read and followed the [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) doc
- [x] I have performed a self-rev

items.title: [#13806] Include enums in the ABI returned by the node API
items.body:
## Description
This PR partially resolves #13806 by adding a description of enum variants to the ABI returned by the node API.

## Prompt
The aptos node API can produce an ABI describing a Move module if you hit `/v1/accounts/<account>/module/<module_name>`, e.g. https://api.mainnet.aptoslabs.com/v1/accounts/0x1/module/coin. Last year we added support for enums to aptos move, but we didn't expose it in the ABI. This means if there is a struct in a Move module and it is an enum, there will be no description of its fields. You can run a localnet like this: `cargo run -p aptos -- node run-localnet` then run this query to see what I mean `curl
http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1/accounts/0x72f0a3b2f254784e2245459602e0c230758b61ab354cd057ad8d15c179d2f18c/module/enums | jq .abi`. See how DrawCommand has no fields in the ABI.

I want you to add full support for enums in the ABI. You'll have to think about this quite hard, make sure you understand the ABI generation stack.

## How Has This Been Tested?
See the updated test in `api/src/tests/move/pack_abi/sources/test.move`. See also that if you deploy this package (https://github.com/banool/move-examples/blob/0f6e9a181094df74d27465949c1bf493223d132b/enums/sources/enums.move) and run this query, you see the enum correctly described:
```
$ curl http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1/accounts/<addr>/module/enums | jq .abi
{
  "address": "0x72f0a3b2f254784e2245459602e0c230758b61ab354cd057ad8d15c179d2f18c",
  "name": "enums",
  "friends": [],
  "exposed_functions": [
    {
      "name": "draw_circle",
      "visibility": "public",
      "is_entry": true,
      "is_view": false,
      "generic_type_params": [],
      "params": [
        "u64"
      ],
      "return": []
    },
    {
      "name": "draw_rectangle",
      "visibility": "public",
      "is_entry": true,
      "is_view": false,
      "generic_type_params": [],
      "params": [
        "u64",
        "u64"
      ],
      "return": []
    }
  ],
  "st

items.title: [gas profiler] major UI overhaul
items.body:
### Summary This delivers a **major UI overhaul** of the gas profiling HTML report. The report is redesigned from a basic, static layout into a modern, interactive page with clearer hierarchy, sortable/exportable tables, a dedicated execution trace view, and refined styling and metadata. ### Before/After ### Changes - **UI / styling** - New CSS theme (accent colors, borders, gradients) and consistent typography/spacing. - Collapsible sections: Summary and subsections use `
` with ▶/▼ indicators. - Section headers restyled (e.g. gradient + left border); tables get header gradient and row hover. - Flamegraph: Aqua palette and `color_diffusion = false` for a cleaner look. - **Tables** - Sortable columns: click header to sort; numeric defaults to descending order while text defaults to ascending. - Copy: “Copy” button to copy the table as tab-separated text. - CSV export: “CSV” button to download the table as a `.csv` file. - **Execution trace** - Trace is rendered into a separate `trace.html` and loaded in the main report via iframe (lazy loading if trace is large). - Button to copy the trace. - **Report metadata** - Footer: git revision and timestamp - **Tests / deps** - New E2E Move test for better test coverage and ease of generating report previews. --- > [!NOTE] > **Low Risk** > Changes are limited to gas profiler report generation/templates and add only new assets/options; main risk is regressions in HTML output structure/asset writing and large-trace handling. > > **Overview** > Delivers a **major overhaul of the gas profiler HTML report**: adds a shared CSS theme, collapsible `
` sections, sortable tables,

items.title: [encrypted mempool] Misc. cleanup of `aptos-batch-encryption` crate
items.body:
<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Mostly cleanup/refactoring, but it touches core batch-encryption ID/digest plumbing and shifts some failures from recoverable `Option/Result` to panics/assumed invariants, so subtle runtime edge cases could surface.
> 
> **Overview**
> Streamlines `aptos-batch-encryption` by removing unused code (`shared/algebra/interpolate.rs`, `shared/ark_serialize.rs`) and dropping the `bytes` dependency from the crate/lockfile.
> 
> Simplifies `IdSet` construction by removing `Option`-returning constructors and updating all call sites/tests to stop unwrapping; `digest()` paths now assume valid inputs and use `expect` messages where invariants should hold.
> 
> Refactors `FPTXSuccinct` and `FPTXWeighted` to delegate shared operations (encrypt/digest/prepare/decrypt/proof computation, etc.) to `FPTX`, and tightens test-only helpers (`Ciphertext::random`, `BIBECiphertext::blank_for_testing`) under `#[cfg(test)]`. Also gates encrypted-transaction test utilities in `aptos-transaction-filters` behind `#[cfg(test)]`.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit cf39acb9e3c2c376d7688974729cb61fb9728794. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

items.title: [storage] Set fill_cache(false) on pruner RocksDB reads
items.body:
Pruners continuously scan stale data via iterators to determine what to
delete. This data is old, about to be deleted, and will never be read
again by the serving path. Without `fill_cache(false)`, every block
touched by pruner iterators is loaded into the shared RocksDB block
cache — evicting hot data that serves user queries.

Changes applied to all pruner iterator call sites:

- `StateMerklePruner::get_stale_node_indices` (covers both metadata and
  shard pruners)
- `StateKvShardPruner::prune`
- `StateKvMetadataPruner::prune` (both sharded and non-sharded branches)
- `TransactionPruner::get_pruning_candidate_transactions`
- `EventDb::prune_event_indices` (inlined iterator to avoid changing the
  shared `get_events_by_version_iter` used by API/backup paths)
- `EventStore::prune_event_accumulator`

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Behavioral change is limited to RocksDB read caching hints during pruning/iteration; data semantics and deletion logic are otherwise unchanged.
> 
> **Overview**
> Pruning-related RocksDB scans now construct `ReadOptions` with `fill_cache(false)` and use `iter_with_opts` so iterator reads of soon-to-be-deleted data don’t pollute the shared block cache.
> 
> This is applied across event accumulator pruning, event index pruning (by inlining an iterator rather than changing `get_events_by_version_iter`), transaction pruning candidate scans, state KV metadata/shard pruning (sharded and non-sharded), and state merkle stale-node index scanning.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 4ca4b26b49718e5e0c34b36103403688676e8365. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

items.title: [node] Reduce default thread counts for backup and index-db runtimes
items.body:
On a 44-core machine, `aptos-node` spawns ~800 threads (~18x oversubscription).
Many tokio runtimes default to `num_cpus` despite having minimal workloads. This
change right-sizes two of the most egregious pools, saving ~84 threads.

- **backup**: 44 → 2 threads. The backup service runs a single `warp` HTTP
  server task that serves infrequent backup requests. 44 worker threads sit
  permanently idle. Add `backup_service_runtime_threads` to `StorageConfig`
  (default: `Some(2)`, configurable).

- **index-db**: 44 → 2 threads. The internal indexer DB service runs a single
  sequential loop that watches for new committed versions and processes them
  synchronously via `db_indexer.process()` on one worker thread at a time.
  Add `runtime_threads` to `InternalIndexerDBConfig` (default: `Some(2)`,
  configurable).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Configuration-only change that adjusts runtime sizing and adds optional knobs; main risk is under-provisioning threads in atypical high-throughput backup/indexer workloads.
> 
> **Overview**
> Reduces thread oversubscription by **right-sizing two Tokio runtimes**: the storage backup service and the internal indexer DB runtime now take an optional configured thread count instead of defaulting to `num_cpus`.
> 
> This introduces `storage.backup_service_runtime_threads` and `indexer_db_config.runtime_threads` (both default to `Some(2)`), wires them through `aptos-node`/`indexer-grpc` runtime creation, and updates backup service call sites/tests to pass the new parameter.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 1727a8e28263dafb21a6f3758f1840a749753b52. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

items.title: Rust 1.92
items.body:
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Upgrades the Rust toolchain and builder images, which can surface new compiler/lint behaviors and subtly affect builds across the workspace. The remaining changes are mostly mechanical refactors to satisfy new warnings and should be low runtime risk.
> 
> **Overview**
> Moves the project to **Rust `1.92.0`** by updating `rust-toolchain.toml`, the Docker builder base (`rust:1.92.0-trixie`), and the image update script.
> 
> Hardens `aptos-node` builds by **failing compilation** if `testing`/`fuzzing` features are enabled in production, replacing the previous runtime assert.
> 
> Applies a set of compiler/lint-driven cleanups across crates: removes `aptos-logger` from `aptos-compression`, refactors multiple `is_err()/unwrap_err()` patterns to `if let Err(err)`/`match`, simplifies iterator usage (dropping unnecessary `.cloned()`), and addresses new warnings (e.g., `#![allow(unused_assignments)]`, lexer parsing tweak, minor variable/unused value fixes).
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 0979d496d12faa24cb3c3cf29e2737a828cf7027. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

items.title: [test] Add encrypted transaction decryption smoke test
items.body:
## Description

Add an end-to-end smoke test that validates the full encrypted transaction lifecycle:

1. Spin up a 4-validator local swarm with DKG and randomness enabled.
2. Wait for epoch 2 and verify an encryption key is present in the ledger info.
3. Use the transaction emitter to generate encrypted traffic and confirm commits.
4. Wait for the next epoch and verify the encryption key rotates.
5. Scan committed transactions to confirm encrypted payloads were included on-chain.

New file: `testsuite/smoke-test/src/decryption.rs`

## How Has This Been Tested?

This *is* the test. Run with:
```
cargo test -p smoke-test -- test_encryption_key_rotation_and_encrypted_txns
```

## Type of Change
- [x] Tests

## Which Components or Systems Does This Change Impact?
- [x] Validator Node

items.title: [Network] Remove old netbench code.
items.body:
## Description
This PR removes the old `netbench` code. This hasn't been used in quite some time, and contains a bunch of legacy code and design decisions that we're cleaning up.

Note: in the future, we'll write a better benchmark for the network stack 😄 

## Testing Plan
Existing test infrastructure.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Removes a config field and associated runtime wiring, which can break users/tests that still set `netbench` in node configs even though the code is largely dead.
> 
> **Overview**
> Removes the legacy `netbench` benchmarking feature end-to-end: the `aptos-network-benchmark` crate is deleted from the workspace, `aptos-node` no longer wires up the netbench network application/service, and the `NetbenchConfig` field and sanitizer are removed from `NodeConfig`.
> 
> Cleans up related test infrastructure by deleting the Forge CLI netbench suite and simplifying multi-region helpers, while leaving `ProtocolId::{NetbenchDirectSend, NetbenchRpc}` as *currently unused* identifiers.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit ff019163312e47cab307a6b7e2f91acc9ed50178. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

items.title: Signed integer support in transactional test arguments
items.body:
## Description

In this PR, we allow signed integers to be passed as args in transactional tests, e.g.,

```
//# run 0xc0ffee::m::test --args -1i64 -12i64
```

Previously, stuff starting with `-` would be considered a flag parsed by clap, thus causing a command error.

## How Has This Been Tested?

- Added a new test to demonstrate the fix.

## Type of Change
- [x] Bug fix

## Which Components or Systems Does This Change Impact?
- [x] Move Compiler

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches core command tokenization and numeric literal parsing used by multiple tools, so subtle regressions in CLI argument parsing are possible despite added tests.
> 
> **Overview**
> Transactional tests can now pass *signed integer literals* via `--args` (e.g. `--args -1i64 -12i64`) by preprocessing task command tokens to rewrite negative values into `--args=...`, avoiding clap treating them as flags.
> 
> The Move CLI value lexer/parser is updated to recognize `i*` numeric suffixes, tokenize negative numeric literals (including hex like `-0xFFi64`), and to **reject untyped negative integers** with a clearer error message. New transactional tests and parser unit tests cover signed argument round-trips and failure cases.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit c5592c0d180d835a9cb4c21809efc850e72f7c2f. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

items.title: [compiler-v2] Fix parsing of signed int returns
items.body:
## Description

Currently, explicit returns of signed integers are not parsed correctly, leading to compiler errors down the line.

For example,

```move
    public fun test(p: bool, q: i64): i64 {
        if (p) return -56 + q;
        -88
    }
```
gives an unexpected type error:
```
 error: cannot return nothing from a function with result type `i64`
  ┌─ TEMPFILE:4:16
  │
4 │         if (p) return -56 + q;
  │                ^^^^^^
```

This PR fixes this error.

## How Has This Been Tested?
- added new test
- modified an existing test to remove `return - 0` (because this causes an error and violates the spirit of the rest of the test)
- existing tests pass

## Type of Change
- [x] Bug fix

## Which Components or Systems Does This Change Impact?
- [x] Move Compiler

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches core expression parsing/precedence handling, so regressions could affect how `-` binds across many syntactic contexts, though changes are well-covered by added/updated tests.
> 
> **Overview**
> Fixes Move compiler v2 parsing so expressions like `return -56 + q` are treated as returning a signed numeric expression rather than a `return` with no value.
> 
> This updates the parser to accept `Tok::Minus` at the start of expressions and implements unary negation in `parse_unary_exp` (folding `-` into numeric literals when possible). Tests are adjusted: the `break` diagnostic note is reworded, `return - 0` is removed from `return_in_binop.move`, and new transactional tests validate returning negative `i64` values (including round-trip decompilation baselines).
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 937fb11bdb12522a38ae920116a9c3e05372cecc. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

items.title: Encrypted transaction context: API, SDK, VM, and execution support
items.body:
## Summary

Add encrypted transaction support across the API, SDK, VM, and execution layers.

### Commits

1. **[api][sdk] Add encryption key header, encrypted transaction builder, and emitter support** — Add `X-Aptos-Txn-Encryption-Key` response header and `encryption_key` field in the JSON index response, exposing the current epoch's encryption key from on-chain state (gated by `allow_encrypted_txns_submission` config). Update the REST client to parse the header. Add encrypted transaction building support to the SDK `TransactionBuilder` and wire the transaction emitter to optionally submit encrypted transactions (disabled by default).

2. **[vm][execution] Support encrypted transaction execution** — Add encrypted transaction handling to the Aptos VM: validate encrypted payloads, execute decrypted transactions with proper nonce checks, and pass decryption context through the block executor and execution pipeline. Update transaction filters and quorum store config for encrypted transaction support (batch v2 and opt QS v2 payload flags default to false).

## Type of Change
- [x] New feature

## Which Components or Systems Does This Change Impact?
- [x] Validator Node
- [x] Full Node (API, Indexer, etc.)
- [x] Move/Aptos Virtual Machine
- [x] Aptos CLI/SDK

items.title: [TSS] add gas profiler support and bump CLI version
items.body:
This adds gas profiling support to Transaction Simulation Sessions. 
- `Session::execute_transaction` now accepts a flag to enable gas profiling. When enabled, it runs the transaction through the Aptos gas profiler and writes an HTML gas report (with assets) to the session output directory

Also bumps the CLI version from 8.0.0 to 8.1.0, in preparation for an upcoming release.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Changes how session-based transaction simulation is executed and adds a new profiler codepath that writes additional on-disk artifacts, which could affect performance or output structure.
> 
> **Overview**
> **Transaction Simulation Session now supports optional gas profiling.** `Session::execute_transaction` takes a new `profile_gas` flag; when enabled it runs via `execute_user_transaction_with_modified_gas_meter` and writes an HTML flamegraph report under `gas-report/` alongside the usual `events.json`/`write_set.json` artifacts.
> 
> The Aptos CLI is bumped to `8.1.0` and the `--session` execution path now allows `--profile-gas` (threaded through `simulate_using_session`/`dispatch_transaction`), with tests updated and a new test asserting `gas-report` output creation.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit b4c23197b20ada05de2059e9fd1ccdc2fddc663e. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>

items.title: Make prepare and decrypt work on individual cts
items.body:
This PR:
* Changes the main trait in `aptos-batch-encryption`
  *  `prepare_ct` and `decrypt` take individual ciphertexts, instead of a slice of ciphertexts
  * `decrypt_individual` is renamed to `decrypt_slow`
* Modifies `decryption_pipeline_builder` in consensus to use `prepare_ct -> decrypt` instead of `decrypt_slow`, to allow for less latency on critical path

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches cryptographic decryption plumbing and consensus transaction decryption flow; mistakes could cause decryption failures or performance regressions, though changes are largely API reshaping and call-site updates.
> 
> **Overview**
> **Refactors the `BatchThresholdEncryption` API** so `prepare_ct` and `decrypt` operate on a single ciphertext/prepared ciphertext (instead of slices), and renames `decrypt_individual` to `decrypt_slow` to clarify it’s the slower convenience path.
> 
> **Updates ciphertext preparation/error handling** by introducing `MissingEvalProofError` (carrying the missing `Id`), making `prepare`/`prepare_individual` no longer return `anyhow::Result`, and storing/exposing the `Id` on `PreparedCiphertext` for better tracking.
> 
> **Integrates the new flow in consensus** (`decryption_pipeline_builder`) by precomputing `PreparedCiphertext`s in parallel before waiting for the reconstructed key, then final-decrypting per txn (with improved error logging on missing proofs), and adjusts benches/tests to use new helpers for batch operations.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit a63c159cf334c94b0bd81ddf735018b8caff8e48. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

items.title: [state-sync] Combine 4 state-sync tokio runtimes into one shared runtime
items.body:
State sync previously created 4 separate tokio runtimes (`sync-driver`,
`stream-serv`, `data-client`, `stor-server`), each defaulting to `num_cpus`
worker threads. On a 44-core machine this means **176 threads** for state
sync alone. These subsystems are mostly I/O-bound and form a single logical
pipeline, so sharing one runtime is both safe and efficient.

Changes:
- Add `num_runtime_threads: Option<usize>` to `StateSyncConfig`
  (default: 16)
- Create a single `state-sync` runtime in `start_state_sync_and_get_notification_handles()`
  and pass its `Handle` to all subsystem setup functions
- Remove the internal `_driver_runtime: Option<Runtime>` from `DriverFactory`;
  change `create_runtime: bool` → `runtime: Option<Handle>`
- Collapse `StateSyncRuntimes` from 3 runtime fields to 1
- Simplify `StorageSynchronizer::new()` parameter from `Option<&Runtime>` to
  `Option<Handle>`

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Changes how core state-sync tasks are scheduled and executed; misconfiguration or handle/lifetime issues could cause startup, performance, or liveness regressions under load.
> 
> **Overview**
> Reduces state sync thread overhead by replacing the separate tokio runtimes for the driver, data client, streaming service, and storage server with a **single shared `state-sync` runtime** that all subsystems spawn onto.
> 
> Introduces `state_sync.num_runtime_threads` (default **16**, `None` = tokio default) to size the shared runtime, updates `DriverFactory`/`StorageSynchronizer` to accept an optional `tokio::runtime::Handle` instead of creating/holding their own runtimes, and adjusts node startup and tests to use the new `StateSyncRuntime` wrapper and initialization flow.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit e8d9ea14318ec3613b47094302d24ce7a79af441. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/

items.title: [consensus] Add decryption pipeline and secret share management
items.body:
## Description

Wire the consensus layer to support encrypted transaction decryption, building on top of the DKG-derived encryption keys from the base branch.

### Commits

1. **[consensus] Add decryption pipeline and secret share management** — Wire consensus to extract DKG-derived encryption keys at epoch boundaries and configure `SecretShareConfig` for transaction decryption. Update decryption pipeline builder, execution client, and state computer. Move `TEST_DIGEST_KEY` from `aptos-dkg-runtime` to `aptos-batch-encryption` to avoid unnecessary dependency. Export `DecryptPrivKey` from `aptos-dkg` and add `ChunkyDecryptPrivKey` type alias in `aptos-types`.

2. **[consensus] Propagate SecretSharedKey to consensus observer via PipelinedBlock** — Store `SecretSharedKey` on `PipelinedBlock` so the observer receives the key via the existing consensus observer protocol. The observer now reads the real `OnChainChunkyDKGConfig` from on-chain configs instead of hardcoding `default_disabled()`.

## How Has This Been Tested?

- `cargo check` and `./scripts/rust_lint.sh` pass clean
- Existing unit tests updated with new parameters
- Integration testing via local validator with encrypted transactions

## Type of Change
- [x] New feature

## Which Components or Systems Does This Change Impact?
- [x] Validator Node
- [x] Full Node (API, Indexer, etc.)

items.title: [enctx] Add encrypted transaction types, DKG encryption key, and framework wiring
items.body:
## Description

End-to-end plumbing for encrypted transactions: from core types through DKG key derivation, Move framework resources, and VM/genesis integration.

### Commits

1. **[types] Refactor DKG output types and add encryption key resource** — Add `CertifiedChunkyDKGOutput`, `AggregatedSubtranscript`, `TranscriptBytesForSigning`, and `PerEpochEncryptionKeyResource` to the types crate. Rework ChunkyDKG public parameters. Refactor `SecretShareConfig`. Enable chunky DKG by default in genesis config.

2. **[types] Add encrypted transaction types** — Add `TransactionExecutable::Encrypted` variant and `into_encrypted_variant()` for signature verification of encrypted payloads. Add `CryptoHasher`/`BCSCryptoHash` derives to `DecryptedPayload`. Update `EncryptedPayload::executable()` to return `Encrypted` instead of erroring.

3. **[dkg] Derive encryption key from DKG transcript** — Derive per-epoch encryption key from the aggregated subtranscript's dealt public key and a test `DigestKey`. Package the key into `CertifiedChunkyDKGOutput`. Add multi-signature verification in the certification step. Fix early return bug in `epoch_manager`.

4. **[framework] Add PerEpochEncryptionKey to decryption module** — Add `PerEpochEncryptionKey` resource to the Move decryption module with `set_for_next_epoch()` and `on_new_epoch()`. Wire into `reconfiguration_with_dkg` and `config_buffer`.

5. **[vm][genesis][api] Wire encryption key through execution and genesis** — Update VM chunky DKG processing to pass encryption key to Move. Add `ENCRYPTED_TRANSACTIONS` feature flag with gating in validator and execution paths. Add genesis initialization. Add `encryption_key` field to API types.

## How Has This Been Tested?

- Manual review of type changes and framework module logic
- Existing DKG and framework tests

## Type of Change
- [x] New feature

## Which Components or Systems Does This Change Impact?
- [x] Validator Node
- [x] Full Node (API, Indexer, etc.)
- [x] Move/Aptos Virtual M

items.title: [storage] Fix HotState race condition via RCU / deferred merge
items.body:
069beddb1e0b relaxed assertions so that fork-induced invariant violations
in `StateSummary::update` return errors instead of panicking. That handles
the *summary* path but does not address the underlying data race in
`HotState`: the `Committer` background thread mutates DashMap entries
one-by-one while concurrent readers observe the same DashMaps, and during
a fork the speculative overlay no longer shields readers from those
in-progress mutations.

### The fork scenario

```
         committed          speculative
            |                    |
            v                    v
  ... ─── A ─── B ─── C         (persisted, Committer working here)
            \
             \── B' ── C'        (fork branch, execution reading here)
```

Without a fork, the speculative overlay covers every key the Committer
touches (same blocks, same LRU decisions), so readers never reach the
DashMap for mutated entries. With a fork, `B'/C'` diverge from `B/C`:
the overlay for the fork branch does not cover keys that only changed
on the committed branch. A reader on the fork path falls through the
overlay into the DashMap mid-mutation and can observe:

```
  Committer thread              Reader thread (fork branch)
  ─────────────────             ─────────────────────────
  remove(key_X) from shard
                                 get(key_X.neighbor) -> LRU next = key_X
                                 get(key_X)          -> MISSING!  💥
  insert(key_X) into shard
```

This causes panics in `HotStateLRU::expect_hot_slot()`.

### Fix: RCU / deferred merge

Decouple *publishing* the new committed state from *mutating* the
DashMaps. The Committer maintains three `State` references:

```
  merged_state ──[delta overlay]──> committed.state <── to_commit
       |                                  |
  base DashMaps                     what readers see
  (physical store)                  (via LayeredHotStateView)
```

- **`merged_state`**: what the base DashMaps currently reflect.
- **`commi

items.title: [TSS] implement new_block + advance_epoch and add tests
items.body:
### Summary

This PR adds **`new_block`** and **`advance_epoch`** to the transaction simulation session (TSS). Sessions can now advance time and trigger block/epoch boundaries by running a `BlockMetadata` transaction through the VM, so simulations can exercise block prologue behavior, reconfiguration, and time-dependent logic.

### Changes

- **Session API (`session.rs`)**
  - **`new_block(timestamp_usecs: Option<u64>)`** — Executes a new block at the given timestamp (or current + 1 μs if `None`). Runs a `BlockMetadata` transaction so the full block prologue runs: updates `CurrentTimeMicroseconds`, emits `NewBlockEvent`, may trigger epoch change if past `epoch_interval`, and updates staking stats. Returns `NewBlockResult` and persists summary/events/write set under `[N] new block`.
  - **`advance_epoch()`** — Advances the simulation to the next epoch by computing the minimum timestamp to cross the epoch boundary (using on-chain `BlockResource.epoch_interval` and `ConfigurationResource.last_reconfiguration_time_micros`) and calling the shared `run_new_block` path.

- **CLI (`crates/aptos/src/move_tool/sim.rs`)**
  - **`aptos move sim new-block`** — `--session <path>` and optional `--timestamp-usecs`
  - **`aptos move sim advance-epoch`** — `--session <path>`

- **Tests** — New tests for `new_block` (timestamps, epoch boundaries, events, randomness) and `advance_epoch` (single/double advance, timestamp/epoch events). Additional tests for session lifecycle, execute, fund, and view operations.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Introduces VM-executed `BlockMetadata` transactions that mutate simulated chain time/epoch state and reconfiguration behavior, which could affect downstream simulation assumptions despite being well-covered by new tests.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds first-class block/epoch progression to transaction simulation sessions by executing a `BlockMetadata` transaction through the VM (`Session::new_block`, `Session::adv

items.title: crypto native cleanup
items.body:
## Description

Better error code and messages.

## How Has This Been Tested?
exising UTs

## Key Areas to Review
n/a

## Type of Change
- [x] New feature

## Which Components or Systems Does This Change Impact?
- [x] Move/Aptos Virtual Machine

- [x] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation

<!-- Thank you for your contribution! -->

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches security-sensitive cryptography natives and changes failure modes from panics to abort codes, which could affect on-chain behavior and tests, though core algorithms and gas charging are largely unchanged.
> 
> **Overview**
> Hardens multiple crypto natives by eliminating panics/`unwrap()`s and replacing them with explicit `SafeNativeError::abort_with_message` error codes/messages, especially around constant loading, type-tag conversion, MSM window sizing, and arkworks/hash-to-curve/MSM operations.
> 
> Notably, algebra constants (`BLS12381_*`, `BN254_*`) are now lazily initialized as `Option` and checked at use sites; Ristretto point storage now validates handles (and duplicate borrows) instead of panicking; and Bulletproofs plus test-only Ed25519/MultiEd25519/BLS12381 signing helpers return structured aborts on invalid inputs or internal failures. The e2e `cryptoalgebra` test is updated to only assert the failure is handled (no longer expecting a panic).
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 14f8528cbc847840cafe2beadfc369c770287a05. This will update automatical

The adapter supports a config parameter, to either show ALL the wallets, or only the ones who support the deeplink property

> Updates mobile connect behavior by fixing the MetaMask deeplink name match (`MetaMask`) and introducing `WalletItem.showAllOnMobile` to optionally render wallets on redirectable/mobile views even without a `deeplinkProvider` (used by the `nextjs-x-chain` wallet selector, which also adds “More wallets” for Sui installables).

items.title: feat(cross-chain-core): add server-side Solana signer for CCTP withdraw claims
items.body:
## Summary

Adds support for server-side Solana claim transactions during CCTP withdrawals (Aptos → Solana). This eliminates the need for users to sign a second wallet transaction after waiting for attestation, providing a seamless one-click withdrawal experience.

## Problem

When withdrawing USDC from Aptos to Solana via CCTP:
1. User signs the Aptos burn transaction ✅
2. SDK waits ~60 seconds for Wormhole attestation ⏳
3. User must sign a **second** Solana claim transaction ❌ (friction point)

The second wallet popup after a long wait creates poor UX and can trigger security warnings in wallets like Phantom.

## Solution

Added `serverClaimUrl` configuration option to `CrossChainDappConfig`. When configured:
- The SDK automatically POSTs the attested receipt to the dapp's server endpoint
- The server signs and submits the Solana claim transaction using a funded keypair
- User only signs once (the initial Aptos burn)

### SDK Changes (`cross-chain-core`)
- Added `serverClaimUrl` option to `solanaConfig` in `CrossChainDappConfig`
- Updated `claimWithdraw()` to detect and use server-side claim path
- Added JSON serialization for `BigInt`, `Uint8Array`, and `UniversalAddress` in receipts

### Demo App Changes (`nextjs-x-chain`)
- Added `/api/claim-withdraw` API route with reference implementation
- Added `SolanaLocalSigner` class for server-side transaction signing
- Added documentation in `SERVERSIDE_SOLANA_SIGNER.md`

## Usage

// Client-side config
```
const crossChainCore = new CrossChainCore({
  dappConfig: {
    aptosNetwork: Network.TESTNET,
    solanaConfig: {
      serverClaimUrl: "/api/claim-withdraw",
    },
  },
});
```
# Server-side env var 
```
SOLANA_CLAIM_SIGNER_KEY=<base58-encoded-private-key>
```

## Benefits

- ✅ **Better UX** — No second wallet popup after attestation wait
- ✅ **Seamless flow** — User only signs once
- ✅ **Reliable** — Server can retry failed transactions
- ✅ **Backwards compati

items.title: Token verification visibility
items.body: ### Description This PR enhances the visibility of token verification status on coin and fungible asset detail pages. It introduces a new `VerificationBanner` component that: - Displays a prominent success banner for verified tokens (Native, Labs Verified, Community Verified). - Shows a clear warning banner for unverified or recognized tokens, featuring an obvious "Get Verified" button that links directly to the token verification page (`/verification`). - Does not display a banner for banned/disabled tokens. The banner is integrated into both the Coin and Fungible Asset detail pages, positioned between the title and the tab content, making the verification status and the path to verification more apparent to users. ### Related Links ### Checklist --- [Slack Thread](https://aptos-org.slack.com/archives/C03SEHX3C1F/p1771611770844169?thread_ts=1771611770.844169&cid=C03SEHX3C1F)
